title: Hyde Park - Long Water
tags: longwater serpentine lake hydepark kensingtongardens centralondon cityofwestminster park london england britain greatbritain unitedkingdom uk europe
description: The Long Water is the western half of the ornamental lake that stretches across Kensington Gardens and Hyde Park in central London. Created in 1730鈥?733 at the request of Queen Caroline, wife of George II, it was designed by royal gardener Charles Bridgeman as part of a major landscape renovation that also introduced the Serpentine to the east. Although commonly perceived as one continuous body of water, the Long Water and the Serpentine are technically distinct, separated by the 19th-century Serpentine Bridge, which connects the two royal parks.

title: Heron  St James's Park London
tags: birds bird heron herons water lake lakes park walk parks london londonengland londonparks ldn avian wildlife stjamespark royalparks england english cities city royllowarch royrichardllowarch parklife feathers outdoor stjamessparklondon greyheron canonphotos canonphotography londonsightseeing londonlandmarks londontourism londonsights londonroyalparks greyherons exploringlondon explorelondon exploringengland exploreengland englishheritage englishtourism waterfowl shadows sunshine greatbritain unitedkingdom stjamessparklake stjamesspark stjamesparklake stjamesparklondon llowarch europe european europeanhistory europeancities centrallondon westminsterlondon travel travelling worldtravel englishhistory vacations staycations holidays grey blue brown orange white black walking lovelondon sunny
description: A walk in the Park. St James's Park Park in London on a beautiful hot day in July 2018. Photo taken with my Canon DSLR Camera. St James's Park in London is a beautiful place to take a walk, sit in the sunshine or shade, watch to waterfowl around the lake and to view Buckingham Palace. The park gets packed out with locals and tourists on beautiful days St James's Park is a 23-hectare (57-acre) urban park in the City of Westminster, central London. A Royal Park, it is at the southernmost end of the St James's area, which was named after a once isolated medieval hospital dedicated to St James the Less, now the site of St James's Palace. The area was initially enclosed for a deer park near the Palace of Whitehall for King Henry VIII in the 1530s. It is the most easterly of a near-continuous chain of public parks that includes (moving westward) Green Park, Hyde Park, and Kensington Gardens. The park is bounded by Buckingham Palace to the west, The Mall to the north, Horse Guards to the east, and Birdcage Walk to the south. It meets Green Park at Queen's Gardens with the Victoria Memorial at its centre, opposite the entrance to Buckingham Palace. St James's Palace is on the opposite side of The Mall. The closest London Underground stations are St James's Park, Green Park, Victoria, and Westminster. description: A walk in the Park. St James's Park Park in London on a beautiful hot day in July 2018. Photo taken with my Canon DSLR Camera. St James's Park in London is a beautiful place to take a walk, sit in the sunshine or shade, watch to waterfowl around the lake and to view Buckingham Palace. The park gets packed out with locals and tourists on beautiful days St James's Park is a 23-hectare (57-acre) urban park in the City of Westminster, central London. A Royal Park, it is at the southernmost end of the St James's area, which was named after a once isolated medieval hospital dedicated to St James the Less, now the site of St James's Palace. The area was initially enclosed for a deer park near the Palace of Whitehall for King Henry VIII in the 1530s. It is the most easterly of a near-continuous chain of public parks that includes (moving westward) Green Park, Hyde Park, and Kensington Gardens. The park is bounded by Buckingham Palace to the west, The Mall to the north, Horse Guards to the east, and Birdcage Walk to the south. It meets Green Park at Queen's Gardens with the Victoria Memorial at its centre, opposite the entrance to Buckingham Palace. St James's Palace is on the opposite side of The Mall. The closest London Underground stations are St James's Park, Green Park, Victoria, and Westminster. The park is Grade I listed on the Register of Historic Parks and Gardens. en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_James%27s_Park

title: St James's Clerkenwell Under High Speed Clouds
tags: clerkenwell st james church england churchyard grade ii listed building night nighttime spire park 1792
description:
Foxes are few and far between and proving uncooperative even when they do appear so the church and churchyard become the subject. 

The few leaves left on the trees give little hint of the breeze, considerably stronger at higher altitude, stretching the clouds over a long exposure. 

A church has stood on this site for more than 1,000 years and this, the latest incarnation, is a whippersnapper not even a quarter of that having been built in 1792, only 3 years after the French Revolution. The Church is a rare Grade II* Listed building
 
The plain brick of the body of the church is in stark contrast to the gleaming white of the Portland stone Spire. Portland stone is common on older buildings throughout London and chosen for churches in particular thanks to its unique ability to be largely self cleaning.

Dark though this picture may be, the reality is darker yet so a little artistic licence has been taken so you can actually see the beauty.
